task_completion.description: Open and read the safety requirements brief from the Desktop.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor or viewer window open displaying the contents of 'safety_hazard_brief.txt'.

task_completion.description: Model at least two 3D hazard zones in the BlenderBIM architectural model.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Blender 3D viewport showing the FZK-Haus model with at least two newly created 3D meshes/volumes (hazard zones) placed within or around the structure.

task_completion.description: Assign the newly created hazard zones to an IFC class.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Bonsai/BlenderBIM Object Properties panel showing an IFC class (such as IfcSpatialZone or IfcBuildingElementProxy) assigned to the selected hazard zone objects.

task_completion.description: Apply the custom property set (Pset_Risk) with the required properties (RiskType and Mitigation).
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Bonsai/BlenderBIM Object Properties or IFC Properties panel displaying a custom property set named 'Pset_Risk' containing the string properties 'RiskType' and 'Mitigation'.

task_completion.description: Apply a material to highlight the hazard zones.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Blender Material Properties panel showing a material assigned to the hazard zones, or the zones visibly appearing colored/highlighted (e.g., red, yellow, or translucent) in the 3D viewport.

task_completion.description: Save the enriched model to the specified output path.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A file export dialog, save prompt, or terminal window showing the file being saved exactly to '/home/ga/BIMProjects/fzk_safety_hazards.ifc'.

integrity.description: The agent used the Blender/Bonsai graphical user interface to complete the task.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show interaction with Blender's 3D viewport, outliner, and property panels rather than just running a python script to generate the IFC file.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the pre-loaded FZK-Haus model.
integrity.visual_evidence: The hazard zones are modeled in the context of the actual FZK-Haus architectural geometry, not in a blank or empty Blender project.

integrity.description: The agent manually created the custom property set and properties using the UI.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the agent typing 'Pset_Risk', 'RiskType', and 'Mitigation' into the Bonsai/BlenderBIM property management UI fields.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The agent must create at least 2 hazard zones. The required custom property set must be named exactly 'Pset_Risk' and must contain the properties 'RiskType' and 'Mitigation'. The final output must be saved to '/home/ga/BIMProjects/fzk_safety_hazards.ifc'. The original fzk_haus.ifc model contains no pre-existing IfcSpatialZone, IfcBuildingElementProxy, or Pset_Risk elements, so any present in the screenshots must have been created by the agent.
